com.sensordata.candidate.test.interfaces

Interface DataFile


  • public interface DataFile
    Interface for a class providing data from a binary file.

    The binary file is in little Endian and is structured as follows:

    Size in bytes Data type Description
    4 int containing the number of signals in the file = N.
    N signal descriptions/meta data, each consisting of:
    len(name) + 1 byte 0 (zero) terminated ANSI ASCII byte string which is the signal name.
    len(unit) + 1 byte 0 (zero) terminated ANSI ASCII byte string which is the signal unit.
    4 int integer designating the data type according to the ordinal of the enums in Enum.ordinal().
    M Data sets: In a round robin manner one value for each signal according to its data type,
    e.g. for the 2 signals Time (for type and size in bytes see DataType.TIME) and Speed DataType.DOUBLE.
    8 long 1st Time stamp
    8 double 1st value of Speed
    8 long 2nd Time stamp
    8 double 2nd value of Speed
    ...
    8 long Mth Time stamp
    8 double Mth value of Speed

    The data access should be realized by an iterator, see read(Signal). The data sets should not all be read into main memory (RAM) in order to also allow processing of files which are bigger than the main memory.

    See Also:
    DataType, Signal

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void close()
      Close currently opened file.
      long getDataCount()
      Return the number (M) of data sets stored in the currently opened file.
      java.util.List<Signal> getSignals()
      Return list of signals in the currently opened file.
      void open(java.io.File file)
      Open the given file and read the meta data, e.g. contained signals.
      java.lang.Iterable<java.lang.Double> read(Signal signal)
      Return Iterable for the given signal to access its data.

    • Method Detail

      • open

        void open(java.io.File file)
          throws java.io.FileNotFoundException,
                 java.io.IOException
        Open the given file and read the meta data, e.g. contained signals. Do not read the data here!
        Parameters:
        file - to open.
        Throws:
        java.io.FileNotFoundException
        java.io.IOException

      • close

        void close()
           throws java.io.IOException
        Close currently opened file.
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • getSignals

        java.util.List<Signal> getSignals()
        Return list of signals in the currently opened file.

      • getDataCount

        long getDataCount()
                  throws java.io.IOException
        Return the number (M) of data sets stored in the currently opened file.
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • read

        java.lang.Iterable<java.lang.Double> read(Signal signal)
        Return Iterable for the given signal to access its data. It is o.k. to return a Long value as an Double. Example usage: 
         System.out.println("Values of " + signal + ":");
 for (Double d : dataFile.read(signal)) {
   System.out.println(d);
 }
        Parameters:
        signal - to read data from.
